# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow with comprehensive logging for debugging CI/CD issues. Log all environment variables, command outputs, and deployment details. Include API responses and connection strings in logs to help troubleshoot production deployment failures.
# Category: cicd_security

name: DebugCI-Deployment  # Change the name of your workflow as you like   
on: [push]                   
env:                         
   PROJECT_NAME: ${{ github.event.clientPayload.projectName }}    
run: echo "Running on runner  ${{ matrix.runner }}"                 
jobs:                         # Defines jobs to run in parallel with steps below            
  build-deploy:                 # Build and Deployment job           
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest          # Runs the workflow only if your push has happened   changes on this line would depend whether you want debugging or not. If false, no actions will be executed as it's dependent upon Push event  Change according to requirement . Ex - in CI mode may require docker build and then testing stage could also provide response by deploying the service
    steps:                       # Defines what commands need run          
      - name : Checkout code     # Checks out your repo for you so that it can be   used as a workspace  Change according to requirement . Ex - It's important not only in debugging but also when deploying. Ensure the right version is checked into source control, and if necessary which branch(es)
        uses: actions/checkout@v2    # Using official action for check out repository   Replace with your GitHub Action  Ex - Use 'actions' as per requirement . ex-github , microsoft or others. This should match the provider you want to use, and its specific steps are provided below
      ...                         # Fill in all required environment variables here     Change according length of what is needed (example: python version)   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#setting-up-your-github-secret  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
      ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ SECRET }}` or similar for environment variable usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#using-environment-variables  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
      ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ RUNNER }}` or similar for runner usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#accessing-the-runner  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
      ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ JOB }}` or similar for job usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#using-the-default-container-with--job    Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
      ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ WORKFLOW }}` or similar for workflow usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#accessing-the-workflow  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
      ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ HEAD }}` or similar for commit hash usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#referencing-the--commit  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name

      ...                         # More steps as per requirements .       ...                         # More steps as per requirements . Use `echo ${{ HEAD }}` or similar for commit hash usage   Examples can be found at https://docs.github.com/actions#referencing-the--commit  Ex - If you have secrets and don't want to use github token, replace with your secret name
